key_insight: MetaDataGenAgent autonomously turns unstructured metamaterials literature into executable structure-response databases using coordinated multimodal agents and a closed-loop plan-execute-reflect workflow.
engineering_relevance: This creates AI-ready metamaterial datasets that can accelerate inverse design, simulation, and deployment of electromagnetic devices in data-scarce engineering domains.
method: Multimodal agents extract structural and response parameters from text and images.
Physics-guided validation and topology-aware analysis refine the parsed metamaterial representations.
Solver-executable encoding converts literature into reusable simulation-ready database entries.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental validation on representative meta-atoms, showing the generated data supports beam deflection, holographic imaging, and topologically protected surface-wave transport.
watch_item: Performance may depend on literature quality, figure interpretability, and the robustness of automated validation across diverse metamaterial layouts.
paper_title: Autonomous Generation of Metamaterial Databases Based on Multimodal Agents
